patches.find: This WIN also triple-counts the same physical phenomenon: WIN-010 (BOU 2017 eclipse), WIN-025 (eclipse geomagnetic perturbations), and WIN-068 (ensemble of 39 eclipses) are three entries for one ionospheric mechanism. Count inflation of this kind is exactly the methodology the review documents under Misleading WINs elsewhere.
patches.replace: WIN-010 (BOU 2017), WIN-025 (2024 9-station), and WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble 1991–2016) invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels. The dome counts each as a separate confirmed prediction, inflating the score count without adding independent evidence.
patches.rationale: Harmonize with sibling WINs (WIN-010, WIN-025) passive framing. A WIN cannot 'triple-count' — the dome author does the counting. Curmudgeon c6 hole 1.

patches.find: The standard model derives all three from the geometry of the Sq current vortex.
patches.replace: The standard model derives all three from the geometry of the Sq current vortex. Crucially, the ionospheric parameters Sq models use (E-region electron density, conductivity tensors) are measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments — not derived from the eclipse signal being predicted. The dome's FSF scaling factor, by contrast, is calibrated directly against the observed eclipse magnetic data it claims to predict, which is circular.
patches.rationale: Preempt 'both sides calibrate' defense (c5 advocate defense_survives=3, c6 still recommends): Sq parameters come from independent instruments; dome FSF is fitted to the eclipse data itself. Curmudgeon c6 hole 2.

patches.find: For a separate internal-consistency concern — the dome's refraction fix leaves the physical sun altitude indeterminate, undermining the geometric shadow it invokes for all 39 eclipses — see Section 1.8.
patches.replace: For a separate internal-consistency concern applicable to the dome's own mechanism (not the Std Model Explains verdict above): the magnetic signal tracks apparent rather than physical shadow geometry, so Chapman's ionospheric mechanism is unaffected by the dome's refraction indeterminacy. But the dome's own claimed cavity mechanism does invoke physical geometry, and the refraction fix leaves physical sun altitude indeterminate — undermining the geometric shadow it claims for all 39 eclipses. See Section 1.8.
patches.rationale: Strengthen inoculation so a defender cannot argue verdict logic undercuts the cross-reference. Clarify this is a dome-mechanism consistency concern only, not a challenge to the Chapman/Std-Model verdict. Option (a) from c6 hole 3 recommendation.

patches.find: Under the dome author's own timestamping definition
patches.replace: Under the dome author’s own timestamping definition
patches.rationale: Add link to Timestamp Error tab. All OTS methodology references should cross-link to the dedicated write-up. Curmudgeon c6 hole 4.
